title: Skeletal Dysplasias and Heritable Connective Tissue Disorders
authors: George E. Tiller & Gary A. Bellus

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Skeletal Dysplasias & Heritable Connective Tissue Disorders?
Skeletal dysplasias พบ 2.14 ใน 10,000 deliveries (Boston series >100,000); หลายรายวินิจฉัยแรกโดย neonatologist ที่ delivery room. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) Thanatophoric dysplasia + OI type II + achondrogenesis type II = 40% ของ prenatal-onset skeletal dysplasias; (2) FGFR3 mutations: achondroplasia (>99% Gly380Arg) → foramen magnum stenosis คือ hidden killer ใน neonates; (3) Cesarean section ไม่ลด fracture rate ใน OI (Bellur Genet Med 2016); (4) Congenital Marfan syndrome: cardiovascular disease affects almost every neonate → early echo + beta-blocker; (5) Menkes syndrome: early copper supplementation ช่วยได้แต่ไม่ 100%; (6) Next-gen DNA sequencing + WES เปลี่ยน diagnostic approach — multigene panels ใช้ได้ตั้งแต่แรกเกิด.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย skeletal dysplasia classification (42 groups) และแยก lethal vs nonlethal ด้วย clinical + radiologic features
รู้ FGFR3 spectrum: achondroplasia, hypochondroplasia, SADDAN, thanatophoric dysplasia — gene, mutation, phenotype severity
รู้ COL2A1 spectrum: SEDC, Kniest, achondrogenesis type II/hypochondrogenesis — collagen type II disorders
รู้ SLC26A2 spectrum: diastrophic dysplasia, achondrogenesis type IB, atelosteogenesis type II — sulfate transport defects
วินิจฉัย OI types II/III ด้วย clinical + radiographic features; รู้ว่า C-section ไม่ลด fracture rate
อธิบาย perinatal hypophosphatasia: ALPL mutation, TNSALP deficiency mechanism, และ enzyme replacement therapy
วินิจฉัย campomelic dysplasia: SOX9 + sex reversal ใน 46,XY; รู้ short rib thoracic dysplasias (ciliopathies)
ระบุ heritable connective tissue disorders ที่ present ใน neonates: congenital Marfan, Beals, EDS types, cutis laxa, Menkes
รู้ว่า FGFR3-targeted therapy (vosoritide) อยู่ใน clinical trials สำหรับ achondroplasia
วางแผน workup skeletal dysplasia ใน NICU: x-ray skeletal survey + genetics consult + molecular testing strategy
1. Overview — Skeletal Dysplasias และ Connective Tissue Disorders
1.1 Definitions
Skeletal dysplasias (osteochondrodysplasias): disorders of cartilage and bone development; incidence 2.14 in 10,000 (Rasmussen Am J Med Genet 1996)
Connective tissue disorders: abnormalities in extracellular matrix components — collagens (>24 types), elastin, fibrillin, microfibril-associated glycoproteins
Latest classification: 42 groups based on radiologic + clinical + molecular criteria (Mortier Am J Med Genet 2019)
Prenatal US: ↑ accuracy → ↑ prenatal diagnoses of both groups
1.2 Genetic Heterogeneity และ Allelic Spectra — หลักการสำคัญ
Mutations ใน same gene → different phenotypes (allelic spectrum): FGFR3 → achondroplasia / hypochondroplasia / SADDAN / thanatophoric dysplasia / Muenke craniosynostosis
Mutations ใน different genes → same phenotype (genetic heterogeneity): multiple epiphyseal dysplasia มีได้หลาย genes

Ciliopathies: disorders affecting primary cilia (microtubule-based organelles for chemosensory/mechanosensory signaling) → short rib thoracic dysplasias; genes: DYNC2H1, IFT80, IFT140, WDR34, EVC, >12 others
1.3 Approach to Diagnosis
Complete physical exam + 3-generation family history + radiographic skeletal survey (skull, thorax rib technique, long bones, hands, feet, pelvis, lateral spine)
Atlases dedicated to skeletal dysplasias: Spranger JW et al. Skeletal Dysplasias Atlas, 3rd ed. 2012 — necessary even for experienced radiologist/neonatologist
Describe limb shortening pattern: rhizomelia (proximal, humerus/femur) / mesomelia (forearm/leg) / acromelia (hand/foot) / micromelia (all segments)

Next-generation sequencing: multigene panels available when clinical + radiographic diagnosis unclear; rapid whole exome sequencing ใน neonatal period ถ้า diagnosis critical for management (Genetic Testing Registry: ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gtr/)
If infant dies undiagnosed: cord blood for DNA + cartilage + skin fibroblasts → histochemical + biochemical + molecular analysis; photos + skeletal X-rays before death if possible

Radiographic Clues ที่ต้องรู้
Platyspondyly (flat vertebrae) = thanatophoric dysplasia, SEDC, Kniest; Wormian bones (skull suture islands) = OI type III, Menkes; Stippled epiphyses = chondrodysplasia punctata; Cloverleaf skull = thanatophoric dysplasia type II; Campomelia (bowed long bones) = campomelic dysplasia; "Elephant ear" iliac wings = achondroplasia; Beaded ribs (callus) = OI type II.
2. FGFR3 Spectrum — Achondroplasia, Thanatophoric Dysplasia, SADDAN

FGFR3 (fibroblast growth factor receptor 3) = membrane-spanning tyrosine kinase receptor. Mutations → constitutive activation → inhibits cartilage growth plate maturation. Spectrum: achondroplasia (mildest) → hypochondroplasia → SADDAN → thanatophoric dysplasia (most severe/lethal).
2.1 Achondroplasia — Most Common Nonlethal Chondrodysplasia
Incidence: 1 in 25,000 live births; autosomal dominant; ~80% sporadic (new mutations); associated with advanced paternal age (mutations of paternal origin) (Goriely Am J Hum Genet 2012)
Mutation: >99% = Gly380Arg (transmembrane domain FGFR3); constitutive activation ↑ inhibition of chondrocyte proliferation

Clinical: rhizomelia (proximal > distal limb shortening) + macrocephaly + frontal bossing + flat nasal bridge + trident hand configuration + joint laxity; thoracolumbar kyphosis ('gibbus') ใน infancy (exacerbated by unsupported sitting)

Key complications: foramen magnum stenosis → spinal cord compression → hypotonia, central apnea, sudden death; obstructive apnea + snoring (small upper airway); spinal stenosis later; hydrocephalus uncommon

Radiology: large calvarium + small foramen magnum + narrow lumbosacral interpedicular space (no flare) + squared iliac wings ('elephant ear') + flat acetabular roofs + short humeri/femurs + longer fibulae + metaphyseal flare + short ribs with anterior cupping

Management: monitor head circumference + plot on achondroplasia growth charts (Hoover-Fong Pediatrics 2020); NO routine brain imaging; hyperreflexia / hypotonia / apnea → MRI for cord compression; rear-facing car seat always; NO sling carriers or umbrella strollers; PT year 1 to strengthen core

Emerging therapy: vosoritide (C-type natriuretic peptide analog → ↓ FGFR3 signaling); Savarirayan Genet Med 2021: 2-yr Phase 3 — safe + persistent growth-promoting effects; trials not yet include neonates/infants
2.2 Thanatophoric Dysplasia — Most Common Lethal Dysplasia
Incidence: 1 in 45,000 births; sporadic; autosomal dominant-acting; may recur due to gonadal mosaicism
Type I: Arg248Cys (most common) → bowed femurs; Type II: Lys650Glu → straight femurs + cloverleaf skull (severe); more severe constitutive FGFR3 activation than achondroplasia

Clinical: extremely short limbs (micromelia) + large head + bulging forehead + prominent eyes + flat nasal bridge + narrow chest + wide fontanel; polyhydramnios; ± cloverleaf skull; ± hydrocephalus + brain anomalies + congenital cardiac abnormalities
Radiology: large calvarium + small foramen magnum + platyspondyly (U-shape or H-shape vertebrae) + short splayed cupped ribs + short bowed long bones + metaphyseal flare with spike
Management: solely supportive — death from pulmonary insufficiency hours to days after birth; genetic counseling + anticipate neonatal death if diagnosed prenatally
DDx: OI types II/III, achondroplasia (severe), achondrogenesis, hypochondrogenesis
2.3 SADDAN และ Hypochondroplasia
SADDAN (Severe Achondroplasia with Developmental Delay and Acanthosis Nigricans): FGFR3 Lys650Met — rare; presentation resembles severe achondroplasia + developmental delay + acanthosis nigricans
Hypochondroplasia: FGFR3 mutations (multiple sites); milder than achondroplasia; limb shortening + macrocephaly less pronounced; often diagnosed in childhood not neonatal period
3. COL2A1 Spectrum — Collagen Type II Disorders

COL2A1 encodes collagen type II — predominant building block of cartilage. Mutations → spectrum from lethal (achondrogenesis type II) to viable (SEDC, Kniest, Stickler). All autosomal dominant (most sporadic) ± 6% recurrence from gonadal mosaicism.
3.1 Spondyloepiphyseal Dysplasia Congenita (SEDC)
Name: spondylo (spine) + epiphyseal (growth plates) + congenita (evident at birth)
Clinical: shortened neck + trunk + limbs + normal-sized hands/feet + flat facial profile; ± cleft palate; ± clubfoot; ± tracheomalacia in infancy
Radiology: ovoid/pear-shaped vertebral bodies; odontoid hypoplasia (critical — C1/C2 instability); absent os pubis ossification; ± coxa vara; platyspondyly more evident with age
Childhood complications: myopia + retinal detachment (semiannual ophthalmology), hearing loss, C1/C2 instability (may need fusion); osteoarthritis early adulthood → hip arthroplasty
NICU alert: intubation → care with cervical spine (odontoid hypoplasia)
3.2 Achondrogenesis Type II / Hypochondrogenesis
Severe end of COL2A1 spectrum: fetal hydrops + maternal polyhydramnios + severely shortened trunk/limbs; fetal or neonatal death from pulmonary hypoplasia
Radiology: large calvarium (normal ossification); platyspondyly + underossification of vertebral bodies; short chest + protuberant abdomen; marked shortening all tubular bones; small iliac wings
Management: solely supportive; genetic counseling prenatally — anticipate neonatal death; recurrence risk ~6% (gonadal mosaicism); DDx: achondrogenesis type I, OI types II/III
3.3 Kniest Dysplasia
COL2A1 mutation; autosomal dominant; large cranium + flat face + large eyes + cleft palate + proximal limb shortening + enlarged joints + flexion contractures
Childhood: tracheomalacia; myopia + retinal detachment; hearing loss; delayed motor development; DDx: SEDC, hypochondrogenesis
4. SLC26A2 Spectrum — Sulfate Transport Defects

SLC26A2 (formerly DTDST) = sulfate–chloride exchanger of cell membrane; affects sulfation of proteoglycans (chondroitin sulfate B) in cartilage extracellular matrix. All autosomal recessive. Spectrum: lethal achondrogenesis type IB → atelosteogenesis type II → diastrophic dysplasia (viable).
4.1 Diastrophic Dysplasia — Viable

Clinical: hitchhiker thumbs (proximally placed + adducted) + clubfeet + cervical kyphosis + joint contractures + very short limbs; cleft palate + micrognathia; cystic ear swelling → 'cauliflower ears' with inflammation + calcification in infancy
Radiology: ovoid 1st metacarpals; narrow L1–L5 interpedicular spaces; scoliosis; mesomelia (short ulnae + fibulae); broad flared metaphyses; premature ossification of rib cartilage

Management: cervical kyphosis may resolve spontaneously — may impede intubation + risk cord compression → monitor closely; auricular cysts → compression mold (NOT incision & drainage) (Cushing Int J Pediatr Otorhinolaryngol 2011); serial casting/surgery for clubfeet; PT for joint mobility; vent if small chest
4.2 Achondrogenesis Type IB — Lethal
Extremely short limbs + large head round face + short nose + small mouth + soft skull + very short neck; hydrops; polyhydramnios; stillborn or dies within hours
Radiology: extremely short long bones (square/globular/triangular shapes + medial femoral metaphyseal spikes); poor ossification calvarium + vertebrae; short ribs
DDx: atelosteogenesis type II (same gene), achondrogenesis type II, hypochondrogenesis; management: supportive only
5. Disorders of Bone Fragility — Osteogenesis Imperfecta (OI) Types II & III
5.1 OI Type II — Perinatal Lethal
Incidence: 1 in 20,000–60,000; 60% stillborn or die day 1; 80% die by 1 month

Clinical: short bowed limbs with circular skin creases + abducted flexed hips + soft boggy head (minimal calvarial bone) + dark blue sclerae + narrow chest; infant cries with handling (multiple fractures at different healing stages)
Radiology: short broad 'telescoped/crumpled' femurs + short bowed tibiae + minimal calvarial mineralization + short wavy ribs with beading (fracture callus) + somewhat flat acetabular + iliac wings
5.2 OI Type III — Progressive Deforming

Less severe than type II; up to 200 fractures in a lifetime; adult height 92–108 cm (microfractures in growth plates); triangular face + bossed broad forehead + tapered pointed chin; blue sclerae initially → white by puberty; large calvarium + large anterior fontanel; Wormian bones; dentinogenesis imperfecta + hearing loss in childhood; severe kyphoscoliosis → cardiopulmonary compromise
Radiology: short deformed femurs (not as crumpled as type II); thin long bones with healing fractures + bowing; undermineralized calvarium with Wormian bones; thin gracile ribs
5.3 Etiology & Inheritance

COL1A1 / COL1A2 mutations (collagen type I — main bone protein); qualitatively abnormal collagen synthesis (not just reduced production) = more severe disease; rare recessive forms: CRTAP, P3H1, PPIB mutations
Inheritance: usually spontaneous dominant-acting mutation; recurrence risk ~6% (parental somatic/gonadal mosaicism); parent usually asymptomatic; autosomal dominant pattern most cases
5.4 Management

⚠️ C-Section ไม่ลด Fracture Rate ใน OI
Bellur S et al. Genet Med 2016: Cesarean delivery is NOT associated with decreased at-birth fracture rates in OI. ดังนั้น mode of delivery ขึ้นกับ obstetric indication ปกติ — ไม่ต้องทำ C-section เพราะ OI อย่างเดียว. OI type II: ไม่คาดว่าจะรอดพ้น neonatal period — palliative care.

OI type III: careful handling — hand behind buttocks + forearm supporting legs; lay on pillow; padded reclined infant seat; bisphosphonates (pamidronate IV) → ↑ bone density + ↓ fracture frequency + possibly prevent short stature; treat only severely affected children (benefit > potential long-term effects) (Rossi Curr Opin Pediatr 2019)
Handling: NO sling carriers + umbrella strollers; car seat padded with egg-crating foam; always in back seat; never carry without full head/neck/hip support
6. Perinatal Hypophosphatasia
Incidence: ~1 in 100,000 live births; lethal; short deformed limbs + soft skull + blue sclerae + undermineralized skeleton (many bones 'absent' on X-ray)
Genetics: ALPL gene mutation → ↓ TNSALP (tissue-nonspecific isoenzyme of alkaline phosphatase); autosomal recessive; 25% recurrence risk

Mechanism: TNSALP normally hydrolyzes inorganic pyrophosphate → phosphate → osteoblastic mineralization. Without TNSALP: extracellular inorganic pyrophosphate accumulates → inhibits hydroxyapatite crystal formation → skeleton fails to mineralize. Also TNSALP needed for pyridoxal 5'-phosphate delivery (vitamin B6 cofactor) → B6-responsive seizures
Labs: serum alkaline phosphatase ↓↓ (key); ↑ inorganic pyrophosphate + ↑ pyridoxal 5'-phosphate in serum; ↑ urinary phosphoethanolamine
Radiology: prenatal polyhydramnios; markedly underossified calvarium + long bones (may appear absent); small thorax; short bowed limbs; spurs in midshaft forearms/lower legs; dense vertebral bodies

Management: enzyme replacement therapy (asfotase alfa) — Hofmann J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2019: Phase 2 open-label study — efficacy + safety in infants/young children with nonlethal hypophosphatasia; previously treatment was supportive only; seizures: pyridoxine responsive
DDx: OI type II, achondrogenesis
7. Other Important Skeletal Dysplasias
7.1 Campomelic Dysplasia — SOX9
Gene: SOX9 (sex-determining region Y–box 9); SOX9 = transcription factor for bone formation + testis development (homologous to SRY)

Clinical: birth length 35–49 cm; bowed legs with midshaft dimples + large anterior fontanel + micrognathia + flat face + cleft soft palate + kyphoscoliosis + narrow chest; sex reversal: 75% of 46,XY males have female external genitalia or ambiguous genitalia; ± absence olfactory bulbs; ± cardiac/renal malformations
Radiology: midshaft campomelia (angulation) of femurs + hypoplastic undermineralized cervical vertebrae + narrow iliac wings + 11 rib pairs (not 12) + scapular hypoplasia
Prognosis: usually lethal in infancy from pulmonary hypoplasia / tracheomalacia / cervical instability; chromosomal studies to determine sex + pelvic US for internal genitalia in all
DDx: OI types II/III, diastrophic dysplasia, kyphomelic dysplasia, thanatophoric dysplasia
7.2 Short Rib Thoracic Dysplasias (Ciliopathies)

Includes: Asphyxiating thoracic dystrophy (Jeune syndrome), Ellis-van Creveld syndrome, short rib–polydactyly syndromes; autosomal recessive; genes: DYNC2H1, IFT80, IFT140, IFT172, WDR19, WDR34, TTC21B, EVC, >12 others
Pathology: primary cilia dysfunction → disproportionate skeletal development + short ribs → pulmonary hypoplasia → respiratory death

Clinical: very short horizontal ribs + variable chest narrowing + short limbs ± postaxial polydactyly; ± congenital cardiac defects (Ellis-van Creveld); ± renal/anal malformations; lethal cases: hydrops + very narrow chest + severe limb shortening
7.3 Chondrodysplasia Punctata — Stippling คือ Key Clue

rows: Cataracts, contractures, ichthyosiform erythroderma, proximal limb shortening
Short humeri/femurs, stippled epiphyses, wide coronal vertebral clefts
Distal phalangeal hypoplasia, severe nasal hypoplasia, cataracts, ichthyosis
Distal phalangeal hypoplasia, stippled epiphyses, paravertebral stippling
Asymmetric rhizomesomelia, cataracts, ichthyosis, patchy alopecia

blocks.text: 'Stippling' = punctate calcifications in epiphyses on X-ray — hallmark; finding stippling → identify type (3 inheritance modes = AR / XLR / XLD)
8. Heritable Connective Tissue Disorders
8.1 Congenital (Early-Onset) Marfan Syndrome

Gene: FBN1 (fibrillin 1); fibrillin = glycoprotein forming microfibrils in aorta, periosteum, cartilage, tendons, pleura, meninges. cMS mutations cluster at exons 24–27 and 31–32; usually sporadic dominant-acting

Clinical: long thin body + aged appearance (lack of subcutaneous tissue + wrinkled sagging skin); dolichocephaly; deep-set eyes ± cataracts; high palate; small pointed chin; arachnodactyly + joint contractures/laxity + clubfoot + adducted thumbs; hypotonia; lenses NOT subluxated at birth

⚠️ cMS: Cardiovascular Disease ใน Almost EVERY Neonate
Congenital Marfan syndrome affects the cardiovascular system in almost every neonate: mitral + tricuspid valve prolapse/insufficiency + aortic root dilation + dilated tortuous ascending aorta. Many die of congestive heart failure in year 1. Echo ทันที + beta-blocker (atenolol) ที่ first signs of aortic root dilation; ± losartan combination (Lacro NEJM 2014; Brooke NEJM 2008). Annual ophthalmologic + cardiac eval throughout childhood.
DDx: Congenital Contractural Arachnodactyly (Beals), autosomal recessive cutis laxa, Loeys-Dietz syndrome
8.2 Congenital Contractural Arachnodactyly (Beals Syndrome — Distal Arthrogryposis Type 9)
Gene: FBN2 (fibrillin 2); autosomal dominant; most = spontaneous mutations; ±gonadal mosaicism

Clinical: arachnodactyly + contractures of large and small joints + crumpled overfolded helices of external ear; thin wasted appearance; cardiovascular: usually only mitral valve prolapse (aortic root dilation rare but possible)
Radiology: elongated proximal phalanges; contractures digits/ankles/knees/hips; thin gracile tubular bones; kyphoscoliosis develops
Management: nutrition for weight gain + PT for joint contractures; surveillance for spinal curvature + aortic root dilation; DDx: cMS, Stickler, homocystinuria, distal arthrogryposis
8.3 Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes (EDS)

Clinically + genetically heterogeneous group; characterized by joint/skin hypermobility + bruising + abnormal wound healing + tissue fragility. 2017 international classification: Malfait Am J Med Genet C 2017;175:8.

rows: Premature delivery (fragile amniotic membranes), floppy, joint laxity, skin hyperelasticity
Fetus at 50% risk; main risk is to pregnant MOTHER (uterine/arterial rupture)
Severe hypotonia + scoliosis at birth + congenital hip dislocation, risk ocular rupture
Fragile soft redundant skin + joint laxity

blocks.text: Management: avoid trauma (skin fragility); wound closure challenging (tendency for dehiscence); most EDS types = autosomal dominant (50% risk per offspring); kyphoscoliotic + dermatosparaxis = AR
8.4 Cutis Laxa

Group of genetically heterogeneous disorders → abnormal elastic fiber assembly/homeostasis. Infantile forms: loose furrowed skin + large anterior fontanel + hypotonia + hernias + congenital hip dislocation
Genes: ELN (mild AD form); ATP7A (X-linked recessive — occipital horn syndrome, allelic with Menkes); FBLN4, FBLN5, ATP6V0A2 (AR forms)
Biochemical clues: ↓ serum copper + ceruloplasmin (X-linked form); abnormal serum sialotransferrin isoelectric focusing (ATP6V0A2)

Childhood complications: developmental delay + pulmonary emphysema + aortic root dilation + arterial tortuosity; annual ophthalmo + cardiac exam; DDx: EDS, Menkes, gerodermia osteodysplastica, de Barsy syndrome
8.5 Menkes Syndrome
Gene: ATP7A (copper-transporting ATPase); X-linked recessive — only males affected; female carriers: pili torti in some hair shafts (lyonization)

Mechanism: ATP7A participates in final processing of copper-dependent enzymes: dopamine β-hydroxylase, tyrosinase, lysyl oxidase (collagen cross-linking), superoxide dismutase, cytochrome c oxidase → impaired collagen cross-linking + pigment production + neurotransmission

Clinical: nonspecific neonatal onset → developmental delay evident 2–3 months; failure to thrive + seizures + severe ocular manifestations; pili torti (kinky sparse hair, hypopigmented, brittle, twisted shafts on light microscopy); early death
Labs: ↓ serum copper + ↓ ceruloplasmin + ↑ plasma dopamine:norepinephrine ratio
Radiology: bladder diverticula (US); tortuous vessels (echo/MRI); Wormian bones + metaphyseal spurring + osteopenia (plain films); gastric polyps (upper GI) — evolves during infancy

Management: early copper supplementation — not effective in all patients; bladder diverticula → urinary retention/UTI → surgical correction; seizure monitoring; bone fragility + vascular fragility → risk hemorrhagic stroke + subdural hematoma (Pinto e Vairo Mol Genet Metab 2019)
9. สรุป Lethal vs Nonlethal Skeletal Dysplasias

rows: Platyspondyly, short cupped ribs, cloverleaf skull (type II)
Rhizomelia, macrocephaly, trident hand, frontal bossing
Small foramen magnum, no lumbar interpedicular flare, elephant ear ilia
Bowed limbs, boggy head, blue sclerae, narrow chest
Crumpled femurs, beaded ribs, minimal calvarial mineralization
Short limbs, triangular face, blue sclerae, Wormian bones
Thin long bones with fractures, Wormian bones
Short neck/trunk/limbs, flat face, normal hands/feet
Platyspondyly, odontoid hypoplasia, absent os pubis
Hitchhiker thumbs, clubfeet, cervical kyphosis, cauliflower ears
Bowed legs, sex reversal (75% 46,XY), narrow chest
Campomelia of femurs, 11 rib pairs, narrow ilia
Soft skull, undermineralized bones, B6-responsive seizures
Extremely short limbs, round face, hydrops
Poor vertebral ossification, medial femoral spikes
Platyspondyly, underossified vertebrae, short tubular bones

blocks.text: 10. NICU Practical — Skeletal Dysplasia / Connective Tissue Disorder ใน NICU

Skeletal survey ทันที: skull + thorax (rib technique) + long bones (AP/lateral) + hands + feet + pelvis + lateral spine — ถ่ายทุก view; ใช้ atlas ช่วย (Spranger atlas) แม้ neonatologist ที่มี experience

Genetics consult วันแรก: complex skeletal dysplasias ต้องการ multigene panel หรือ WES; molecular testing อาจใช้เวลาหลาย weeks — เก็บ DNA (cord blood หรือ EDTA blood) ทันทีถ้า neonate status critical; ถ้า die before diagnosis: skin fibroblasts + cartilage biopsy → frozen
Airway alert ใน SEDC: odontoid hypoplasia → ระวัง C-spine manipulation ตอน intubation; manual inline stabilization; confirm odontoid on X-ray ก่อน elective procedure

OI — safe handling protocol: hand behind buttocks + forearm supports legs; carry on pillow; padded reclined infant seat; NO sling carriers, umbrella strollers, or unsupported holding; analgesia for pain; C-section ไม่จำเป็นเพียงเพราะ OI alone (Bellur 2016)
Hypophosphatasia suspect: serum alkaline phosphatase ↓↓ = key lab; ± B6-responsive seizures → pyridoxine trial; enzyme replacement (asfotase alfa) available — consult genetics/metabolics early

Achondroplasia neonates: hypotonia → delayed motor milestones + head heavy → NO flexed carrying positions; watch for central apnea (foramen magnum compression); if hyperreflexia / apnea / hypotonia → MRI with CSF flow study in flexion + extension; refer to AAP health supervision guidelines (Hoover-Fong Pediatrics 2020)

Campomelic dysplasia: karyotype ทุก case (sex reversal ใน 46,XY); pelvic US for internal genitalia; cleft palate → NG feeds; clubfeet → casting; comfort care ที่สุดใน most; unstable C-spine ใน survivors

Congenital Marfan syndrome: echo ทันที (mitral/tricuspid prolapse + aortic root dilation); atenolol เริ่มที่ first evidence of aortic root dilation; annual ophthalmic + cardiac from birth; screen for scoliosis

Menkes disease suspect: ↓ serum copper + ↓ ceruloplasmin + pili torti (light microscopy) + neurologic deterioration; copper supplementation เร็วที่สุด — แต่ไม่ effective ทุกราย; bladder US for diverticula; refer to neurology + genetics
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Platyspondyly + Micromelia + Large Cranium = Thanatophoric Dysplasia
Thanatophoric dysplasia คือ most common lethal skeletal dysplasia. Key radiographic triad: platyspondyly (U-shape vertebrae) + very short ribs + micromelia (all segments). Type II มี cloverleaf skull. Gly380Arg = achondroplasia; Arg248Cys = thanatophoric type I; Lys650Glu = thanatophoric type II — same gene, different mutations, different severity. Management: comfort care + genetic counseling.

PEARL 2 — Infant ร้องเวลาเปลี่ยนผ้าอ้อม + Blue Sclerae = OI จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
OI type II/III: crying with handling = multiple fractures at different healing stages. Blue sclerae = thin sclera (underlying choroid shows through). Beware: C-section ไม่ลด fracture rate (Bellur Genet Med 2016). Type III: treat with pamidronate IV (bisphosphonate) ใน severely affected children; ไม่ใช่ทุกราย. Recurrence risk: ~6% (gonadal mosaicism).

PEARL 3 — Achondroplasia: Foramen Magnum คือ Silent Threat ใน Year 1
Small foramen magnum in achondroplasia → lower brainstem/cervical cord compression: hypotonia + central apnea + hyperreflexia → rarely sudden death. ไม่ต้อง routine imaging — แต่ถ้า apnea / hyperreflexia / ↓ motor: MRI + CSF flow study in flexion/extension ทันที. ห้าม NO flexed carrying positions (sling/umbrella stroller) ตั้งแต่แรก.

PEARL 4 — Hitchhiker Thumb + Cauliflower Ears = Diastrophic Dysplasia
Hitchhiker thumb (proximally placed + abducted) + talipes equinovarus = diastrophic dysplasia (SLC26A2, AR). Early management: auricular cysts → compression mold เท่านั้น (NOT I&D); cervical kyphosis อาจ impede intubation + cord compression — monitor but often resolves; serial casting for clubfeet. แยกจาก SMA ด้วย joint contractures + cauliflower ears.

PEARL 5 — 46,XY Female Phenotype + Bowed Legs + Narrow Chest = Campomelic Dysplasia
SOX9 regulates both bone formation AND testis development. 75% ของ 46,XY fetuses มี female external genitalia → karyotype ทุกราย campomelic dysplasia. Radiology hallmark: campomelia (midshaft bowing of femurs) + only 11 rib pairs + scapular hypoplasia. Prognosis: usually lethal — pulmonary hypoplasia / tracheomalacia / C-spine instability.
12. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Rasmussen SA et al. Am J Med Genet 1996;61:49: Epidemiology of osteochondrodysplasias — incidence 2.14 in 10,000 deliveries in Boston series >100,000; changing trends due to prenatal US advances.

Mortier GR et al. Am J Med Genet A 2019;179:2393 (Nosology 2019): Latest revision of skeletal dysplasia classification into 42 groups based on radiologic, clinical, molecular criteria — current gold standard for classification.

Bellur S et al. Genet Med 2016;18:570 (BBD Consortium): Cesarean delivery NOT associated with decreased at-birth fracture rates in OI — mode of delivery should follow obstetric indications; changes counseling paradigm.
Rossi V, Lee B, Marom R. Curr Opin Pediatr 2019;31:708: OI — advances in genetics and treatment; review of bisphosphonate therapy (pamidronate) indications + recessive OI forms (CRTAP, P3H1, PPIB).

Hoover-Fong J, Scott CI, Jones MC. Pediatrics 2020;145:e20201010: AAP Committee on Genetics: health supervision for people with achondroplasia — foramen magnum monitoring, growth charts, C-spine decompression indications.

Savarirayan R et al. Genet Med 2021;23:2443: Vosoritide (C-type natriuretic peptide analog — ↓ FGFR3/tyrosine kinase pathway) in achondroplasia — safe + persistent growth-promoting effects at 2-year follow-up Phase 3 extension; not yet for neonates/infants.

Krakow D et al. Am J Med Genet A 2008;146A:1917: Retrospective analysis 1500 prenatal-onset skeletal dysplasias — OI type II, thanatophoric dysplasia, achondrogenesis type II = top 3 diagnoses (nearly 40% combined).

Krakow D, Lachman RS, Rimoin DL. Genet Med 2009;11:127: Guidelines for prenatal diagnosis of fetal skeletal dysplasias — systematic approach to prenatal US + postnatal radiographs for differentiation.

Hofmann CE et al. J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2019;104:2735: Asfotase alfa (enzyme replacement for TNSALP deficiency) in infants/young children with hypophosphatasia — Phase 2 open-label: efficacy + safety confirmed.

Lacro RV et al. NEJM 2014;371:2061: Atenolol versus losartan in Marfan's syndrome — losartan not superior to atenolol but both showed benefit for aortic root dilation; combination used in severe cases.
Brooke BS et al. NEJM 2008;358:2787: Angiotensin II blockade (losartan) and aortic root dilation in Marfan's syndrome — mechanistic rationale via TGF-β pathway; forms basis for losartan use in cMS.
Malfait F et al. Am J Med Genet C 2017;175:8: 2017 international classification of Ehlers-Danlos syndromes — 13 subtypes; provides framework for gene-phenotype correlations and management guidelines.
Pinto e Vairo F et al. Mol Genet Metab 2019;126:6: Systematic review + evidence-based guideline for Menkes disease — early copper supplementation + bladder surveillance + developmental interventions.

Cushing SL et al. Int J Pediatr Otorhinolaryngol 2011;75:713: Prevention of auricular deformity in diastrophic dysplasia — compression mold (NOT incision/drainage) is correct management of auricular cysts.
13. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Neonate มี micromelia (ทุก segment), large cranium, platyspondyly บน X-ray, polyhydramnios ขณะ prenatal — diagnosis ที่เป็นไปได้มากที่สุด? gene? prognosis?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: thanatophoric dysplasia (FGFR3) — most common lethal skeletal dysplasia. Platyspondyly (U/H-shape vertebrae) + micromelia + short cupped splayed ribs + large calvarium กับ small foramen magnum = classic. Type I: Arg248Cys (bowed femurs); Type II: Lys650Glu (straight femurs + cloverleaf skull). Prognosis: lethal — pulmonary insufficiency hours to days. Management: supportive + genetic counseling + anticipate neonatal death. Recurrence: gonadal mosaicism possible — prenatal diagnosis available if mutation identified.
Q: Achondroplasia neonate: ทำไม NO sling carrier/umbrella stroller? เมื่อไหร่ควร MRI?

ตอบ: Sling carriers/umbrella strollers place infant in flexed position → worsens thoracolumbar kyphosis + risks cervical cord compression at small foramen magnum. Thoracolumbar kyphosis exacerbated by unsupported sitting before truncal muscles strong. MRI indication: hyperreflexia / hypotonia / central apnea / apnea / quadriparesis → herald cord compression → MRI with CSF flow studies in flexion + extension; decompression surgery at foramen magnum or upper cervical spine prevents neurologic damage (most gain milestones late but spontaneously as foraminal diameter expands).
Q: OI type II vs OI type III — แยกอย่างไร? bisphosphonate ใช้เมื่อไหร่?

ตอบ: OI type II: perinatal lethal; crumpled femurs; 80% die by 1 month. OI type III: progressive deforming, not immediately lethal; deformed but not crumpled femurs; triangular face; Wormian bones; up to 200 fractures lifetime. Bisphosphonate (pamidronate IV): for severely affected OI type III children — ↑ bone density + ↓ fracture frequency + ↓ pain + possibly prevent short stature; weigh clinical benefit vs potential long-term effects; NOT used in OI type II (lethal). C-section does NOT decrease fractures (Bellur 2016).
Q: Perinatal hypophosphatasia — mechanism, key lab, ทำไมมี B6-responsive seizures?

ตอบ: Mechanism: ALPL mutation → ↓ TNSALP → extracellular inorganic pyrophosphate accumulates → inhibits hydroxyapatite crystal formation → skeleton cannot mineralize. TNSALP also required for pyridoxal 5'-phosphate delivery into cells (cofactor vitamin B6) → ↓ intracellular B6 → B6-responsive seizures. Key lab: serum alkaline phosphatase ↓↓ (remember: ALP normally HIGH in neonates — if ALP paradoxically low in a severely ill neonate → hypophosphatasia). Treatment: asfotase alfa (enzyme replacement). DDx: OI type II, achondrogenesis.
Q: Campomelic dysplasia: ทำไมต้อง karyotype ทุกราย? diagnosis และ management?

ตอบ: SOX9 mutations disrupt both bone formation AND testis determination: SOX9 homologous to SRY. 75% of 46,XY fetuses have female external genitalia or ambiguous genitalia (sex reversal). ดังนั้น external genitalia ไม่สะท้อน chromosomal sex — karyotype ทุกราย + pelvic US for internal genitalia. Diagnosis: bowed femurs (campomelia) + 11 rib pairs + scapular hypoplasia + narrow dolichocephalic skull. Management: comfort care (usually lethal); cleft palate repair if oral feeding possible; C-spine care in survivors.
Q: Congenital Marfan syndrome: ลักษณะทางคลินิกที่แยกจาก classic Marfan? management ใน NICU?

ตอบ: Congenital (early-onset) Marfan: aged appearance (lack subcutaneous tissue + wrinkled skin) + arachnodactyly + joint contractures (NOT just laxity) + crumpled ears; lenses NOT subluxated at birth (develops later). Classic Marfan: ectopia lentis + dolichostenomelia + more aortic disease in adults. Cardiovascular involvement in almost every cMS neonate: mitral/tricuspid prolapse + aortic root dilation. NICU: echo day 1 + atenolol at first sign of aortic dilation + ± losartan; annual ophthalmologic + cardiac eval; screen for scoliosis. Gene: FBN1 (exons 24–27, 31–32 hotspots). DDx: Beals syndrome (FBN2) — crumpled ears + less severe cardiac.
Q: Menkes syndrome ใน neonate — ลักษณะ hair, lab สำคัญ, และ management?

ตอบ: Hair: pili torti (kinky, sparse, hypopigmented, brittle, twisted shafts on light microscopy). Labs: ↓ serum copper + ↓ ceruloplasmin + ↑ plasma dopamine:norepinephrine ratio. X-linked recessive → only males affected; female carriers: pili torti via lyonization. Mechanism: ATP7A mutation → copper enzyme dysfunction → impaired collagen cross-linking + pigment production + neurotransmission. Management: early copper supplementation (not effective in all); seizure monitoring; bladder diverticula → UTI risk → surgical correction; developmental interventions. DDx: cutis laxa (X-linked form = ATP7A allele), EDS, nutritional copper deficiency.
Q: Diastrophic dysplasia: how to manage auricular cysts? ทำไม avoid I&D? cervical kyphosis?

ตอบ: Auricular cysts in diastrophic dysplasia: compression mold application — NOT incision and drainage. Reason: I&D → inflammation → accelerated calcification → severe permanent 'cauliflower ear' deformity (Cushing 2011). Cervical kyphosis: may impede endotracheal intubation + risk cord compression — but often resolves spontaneously in infancy; monitor carefully. For intubation: anticipate difficulty + prepare video laryngoscope/fiberoptic backup. SLC26A2 gene (same as achondrogenesis type IB + atelosteogenesis type II) = AR.
Day 91 Preview

Avery Chapter 91 — Newborn Skin: Development, Structure, and Function
พรุ่งนี้: Newborn Skin Development, Structure, and Function — หลังจาก Day 90 ที่เรียน skeletal + connective tissue disorders ซึ่งสัมพันธ์กับ extracellular matrix (collagens, elastin, fibrillin) วันพรุ่งนี้จะขยายไปที่ skin itself: barrier function, stratum corneum development, gestational age–dependent skin maturity, TEWL (transepidermal water loss) ใน preterm, neonatal skin microbiome, skin care practices ที่ NICU — foundation สำหรับ Days 92–95 (congenital skin disorders, infections, neonatal dermatoses, vascular anomalies).
